steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The second sentence claims that “time did not stand still during these intervening years”—explaining why the material is missing. This is not contradicting the first sentence; it's the natural explanation for the observed missing material.

steps.title: Apply the transition
steps.content: ”Of course” signals that geological activity during the intervening years is the natural, expected explanation for the missing material. The other three choices fail: “On the contrary” wrongly suggests contradiction, “As a result” reverses the causality, and “In particular” introduces an irrelevant detail. That matches choice B — B is correct.
